summary: The one-command closure owner is correctly assigned to run-session, adds no run-session-to-cli reverse dependency, and improves target-model conformance from 81 to 80 violations.
inspection_summary: Confirmed HEAD 66131d96a869fc317519a66c13ea0e3b0b8d3e1d; compared the target model, architecture/spec contracts, run-session wiring, focused tests, and origin/main/current conformance artifacts. The new owner is listed under run-session and uses injected boundaries rather than importing CLI; the existing run-session-to-cli conformance edge count remains 9 in both artifacts, so this change adds none.
judgment_delta: Initial concern: the closure composition could put orchestration policy in CLI or introduce a run-session-to-cli reverse dependency. Final conclusion: target-model.json places src/one-command-pr-ready-closure.js and src/agent-runtime-connectors.js in run-session; the new owner has only node built-in imports and callback-injected boundaries, and direct source inspection found no cli.js import from the run-session owner set.

Initial concern: the conformance improvement could mask a new dependency. Final conclusion: baseline has 81 violations (66 undeclared dependencies, 5 budget violations, 10 orphans) and current has 80 (66, 5, 9); the existing run-session-to-cli finding is 9 edges in both, while agent-runtime-connectors.js is no longer orphaned. Therefore the change improves the total by one without adding a reverse edge.
